summary: Verified Python backend date format fix in Excel/PDF exports. All dates now show as DD-MM-YYYY format (e.g., 15-01-2025) instead of YYYY-MM-DD. Tested cashbook, entries, truck payments, and party summary Excel exports - all passed.

ui_verification.export_buttons: PASS - Excel, PDF, Preview buttons visible

critical_code_review_comments: Date format utility (fmt_date) correctly converts YYYY-MM-DD to DD-MM-YYYY
report_helper.py uses fmt_date for all date type columns via fmt_val function
report_config.json has date columns properly configured with type='date'
All Excel export endpoints use get_entry_row which applies fmt_date automatically
context_for_next_testing_agent: Date format fix verified working in Python backend. The fmt_date utility in /app/backend/utils/date_format.py converts YYYY-MM-DD to DD-MM-YYYY. All export endpoints (cashbook, entries, truck payments, party summary) use this via report_helper.py. Desktop/local JS backends were also fixed but run on user's machine - not testable here.

python_backend.report_helper: /app/backend/utils/report_helper.py - fmt_val() uses fmt_date for date type columns

implementation_verification.whats_new_dialog: App shows v88.19.0 with 'Date format globally DD-MM-YYYY kar diya sabhi PDF/Excel mai' confirming the fix
